structuralIssues.title: EN 13813 AVCP mismatch with CMG
structuralIssues.description: EN 13813 (screed material) appears in both FLO and CMG with different AVCP values. FLO records '1, 3, 4' while CMG records '3, 4'. The FLO entry includes System 1 for reaction-to-fire testing, which is correct per Commission Decision 97/808/EC. CMG likely has an incomplete AVCP entry.
structuralIssues.action_required: Verify EN 13813 AVCP against Commission Decision 97/808/EC. Update CMG entry to include System 1 if confirmed.

structuralIssues.title: EN 14041 standardisation crisis gap
structuralIssues.description: EN 14041:2018 was published by CEN but its OJ citation status is unclear. The 2004/AC:2006 version remains the OJ-cited edition. This is a standardisation crisis gap where CEN publishes a revision but the Commission does not cite it. EPLF is working on further revision. Data notes this correctly.
structuralIssues.action_required: Monitor EN 14041 OJ citation status. When resolved, update standards[] citation field.

structuralIssues.title: Three content sections empty
structuralIssues.description: content.stakeholder_notes, content.key_risks, and content.sources_summary are empty.
structuralIssues.action_required: Populate with proposed content from deep-dive report §8.

structuralIssues.title: Split EPD readiness across 6 material types — most varied DPP landscape of any CPR family
structuralIssues.description: Ceramics: excellent (Confindustria Ceramica industry EPD covers 82.6% Italian production). Laminate: good (EPLF EPDs since 2009, 55% global market). Natural stone: emerging (EUROROC promotes, individual quarry EPDs, lifecycle advantage over ceramics/cement). Vinyl: partial (VOC/chemical data focus, not full EN 15804 EPDs). Wood: moderate (linked to STP, EN 14342). Screed: minimal (EN 13813 cross-listed with CMG). DPP implementation will proceed at different speeds for different materials.
structuralIssues.action_required: Document split EPD readiness in content. Note material-specific DPP implementation timelines.

structuralIssues.title: $52-62B market, 8 TCs, 6+ material types — most diverse CPR family, DPP architecture challenge
structuralIssues.description: European flooring market $52-62B (2024), ~3 billion m²/yr. Material breakdown: ceramics 34%, carpets 34%, laminate 15%, vinyl ~10%. Spans 8 CEN TCs — most of any family. Each material has different production processes, environmental profiles, and EPD methodologies. SReq must coordinate across 8 TCs. DPP data architecture must accommodate radically different product types within one family framework.
structuralIssues.action_required: Include market scale and TC coordination complexity in content.key_risks.

structuralIssues.title: Vinyl flooring: REACH + CPR dangerous substances + VOC emissions — compound regulatory burden
structuralIssues.description: Vinyl (PVC) flooring faces compound regulation: REACH restricts SVHC phthalates (DEHP), industry shifted to DINCH/DEHA alternatives. New CPR 2024/3110 dangerous substances requirements (CEN/TC 351) will require VOC emission declarations. German AgBB/DIBt method is current benchmark (3/7/14-day VOC protocols). DPP for vinyl must include: VOC emission class, phthalate content, indoor air quality data — alongside traditional performance. This cross-regulatory burden is unique to vinyl within FLO.
structuralIssues.source: EU flooring standards (https://globalfloorings.com/2025/08/11/eu-flooring-standards-and-ce-marking-for-environmental-compliance/); PMC DEHP-free PVC (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6856815/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document vinyl cross-regulatory burden in content. Flag CEN/TC 351 dangerous substances as DPP data requirement.
